contents.value: **import** — Flow9 keyword.
**export** — Flow9 keyword.
**if** — Flow9 keyword.
**else** — Flow9 keyword.
**switch** — Flow9 keyword.
**println** — Built-in function/command in Flow9.
**i2s** — Built-in function/command in Flow9.
**d2s** — Built-in function/command in Flow9.
**s2i** — Built-in function/command in Flow9.
**s2d** — Built-in function/command in Flow9.
**let** binds a value to a name (immutable by default).
**match** performs pattern matching against a value.
**type** defines a new type alias or algebraic data type.
A **record** groups named fields into a product type.
A **variant** (sum type) represents one of several possible shapes.
A **function** maps input values to output values. First-class in functional languages.
**Recursion** occurs when a function calls itself to solve smaller subproblems.
**Pattern matching** destructures values and dispatches based on shape.
A **tuple** is a fixed-size product of heterogeneous values.
**Immutable** values cannot be changed after creation.
A **higher-order function** takes functions as arguments or returns them.
**Currying** transforms a multi-argument function into a sequence of single-argument functions.
A **monad** is an abstraction for sequencing computations with context (e.g., Option, Result).
**fold** (reduce) combines elements of a collection into a single result.
**map** applies a function to each element of a collection, producing a new collection.

```flow9
[T]
```
The array type in Flow9 represents an immutable, ordered sequence of elements of the same type. Arrays are the primary collection type and are created using bracket syntax [1, 2, 3]. Since Flow9 arrays are persistent, operations that modify content return new arrays while leaving the original unchanged.

```flow9
Tree<K, V>
```
The Tree type in Flow9 is an immutable balanced binary search tree (similar to a sorted map/dictionary) that maps keys to values. Trees provide O(log n) lookup, insertion, and deletion. They are the standard key-value data structure when ordered access or efficient lookup by key is needed.

```flow9
string
```
The string type in Flow9 represents an immutable sequence of Unicode characters. Strings support concatenation with the + operator and various built-in functions for searching, splitting, trimming, and converting. String literals are enclosed in double quotes with standard escape sequences.

```flow9
ref T
```
A mutable reference cell in Flow9 that holds a value of type T and can be updated in place. Refs are the primary mechanism for mutable state in the otherwise functional Flow9 language. Created with ref(value), read with ^refVar (dereference), and updated with refVar := newValue.

```flow9
Maybe<?> ::= None | Some(value : ?)
```
A union type representing an optional value that may or may not be present. Maybe is used throughout Flow9 for safe handling of possibly-absent values, particularly as return types from lookups in trees and arrays. Pattern matching with switch is used to extract the contained value.

```flow9
int
```
A 32-bit signed integer type in Flow9 for representing whole numbers. Supports standard arithmetic operations (+, -, *, /, %), comparison operators, and bitwise operations. Integer division truncates toward zero.

```flow9
double
```
A 64-bit double-precision floating-point number type in Flow9 for representing decimal numbers. Follows IEEE 754 standard. Supports standard arithmetic operations and mathematical functions from the math module.

```flow9
bool
```
A Boolean type in Flow9 with two possible values: true and false. Supports logical operators && (and), || (or), and ! (not). Used in conditional expressions, filter predicates, and control flow statements.

```flow9
List<T>
```
Immutable linked list. The primary collection type in Flow9.

```flow9
filter(a: [?], fn: (?) -> bool) -> [?]
```
Returns array of elements where fn returns true.

```flow9
iter(a: [?], fn: (?) -> void) -> void
```
Iterates over each element of the array applying fn.

```flow9
iteri(a: [?], fn: (int, ?) -> void) -> void
```
Iterates over each element with its index.

```flow9
mapi(a: [?], fn: (int, ?) -> ??) -> [??]
```
Maps with index.

```flow9
length(a: [?]) -> int
```
Returns the length of an array.

```flow9
concat(a: [?], b: [?]) -> [?]
```
Concatenates two arrays.

```flow9
sort(a: [?], cmp: (?, ?) -> int) -> [?]
```
Sorts an array using the comparison function.

```flow9
exists(a: [?], fn: (?) -> bool) -> bool
```
Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.

```flow9
forall(a: [?], fn: (?) -> bool) -> bool
```
Returns true if all elements satisfy the predicate.

```flow9
find(a: [?], fn: (?) -> bool) -> Maybe<?>
```
Finds the first element that satisfies the predicate.

```flow9
makeTree() -> Tree<?, ??>
```
Creates an empty balanced binary tree (map).

```flow9
setTree(tree: Tree<?, ??>, key: ?, value: ??) -> Tree<?, ??>
```
Inserts or updates a key-value pair in the balanced tree.

```flow9
lookupTree(tree: Tree<?, ??>, key: ?) -> Maybe<??>
```
Looks up a value by key in the balanced tree.

```flow9
removeFromTree(tree: Tree<?, ??>, key: ?) -> Tree<?, ??>
```
Removes a key-value pair from the tree.

```flow9
makeSet() -> Set<?>
```
Creates an empty set.

```flow9
insertSet(set: Set<?>, val: ?) -> Set<?>
```
Inserts a value into the set.

```flow9
containsSet(set: Set<?>, val: ?) -> bool
```
Checks if the set contains the value.

```flow9
strlen(s: string) -> int
```
Returns the length of a string.

```flow9
strContainsAt(s: string, pos: int, sub: string) -> bool
```
Checks if s contains sub at position pos.

```flow9
substring(s: string, start: int, len: int) -> string
```
Returns a substring starting at start with length len.
